questions.text: Which type of tissue contracts to produce movement?
questions.explanation: Muscular tissue is specialized for contraction and movement.

questions.text: Blood is classified as which type of tissue?
questions.explanation: Blood is a fluid connective tissue that transports nutrients and gases.

questions.text: Neurons are cells that belong to which tissue type?
questions.explanation: Neurons are the functional cells of nervous tissue.

questions.text: Which tissue forms the outer covering of body surfaces?
questions.explanation: Epithelial tissue covers and protects body surfaces and cavities.

questions.text: Which muscle type is involuntary and found in internal organs?
questions.explanation: Smooth muscle works involuntarily in organs like the intestines and blood vessels.

questions.text: Which tissue supports and binds other tissues in the body?
questions.explanation: Connective tissue provides structural support and binds tissues together.

questions.text: The tissue that provides a covering or a lining for some part of the body is:
questions.explanation: Epithelial cells are compactly packed with little intercellular matrix.

questions.text: Simple epithelium composed of a single thin layer of flattened cells with irregular boundaries is:
questions.explanation: It is found in the walls of blood vessels and air sacs of lungs.

questions.text: Cuboidal epithelium is commonly found in:
questions.explanation: Its main functions are secretion and absorption.

questions.text: Columnar epithelium consists of tall and slender cells whose nuclei are located at the:
questions.explanation: They are found in the lining of stomach and intestine.

questions.text: If the columnar or cuboidal cells bear cilia on their free surface they are called:
questions.explanation: They function to move particles or mucus in a specific direction over the epithelium.

questions.text: The junction that helps to stop substances from leaking across a tissue is:
questions.explanation: Tight junctions provide a seal between cells.

questions.text: Gap junctions facilitate the cells to communicate with each other by connecting the ________ of adjoining cells.
questions.explanation: They allow rapid transfer of ions, small molecules and sometimes big molecules.

questions.text: The most abundant and widely distributed tissue in the body of complex animals is:
questions.explanation: They link and support other tissues/organs.

questions.text: In all connective tissues except ________, the cells secrete fibres of structural proteins called collagen or elastin.
questions.explanation: Blood is a fluid connective tissue without fibres.

questions.text: Which tissue serves as a support framework for epithelium and is located beneath the skin?
questions.explanation: Areolar tissue is a loose connective tissue.

questions.text: Adipose tissue is a type of loose connective tissue specialized to store:
questions.explanation: The excess of nutrients not used immediately are converted into fats and are stored in this tissue.

questions.text: Tendons and Ligaments are examples of:
questions.explanation: Tendons attach muscles to bones and ligaments attach one bone to another.

questions.text: The intercellular material of cartilage is solid and pliable and resists compression. Cells of this tissue are called:
questions.explanation: Chondrocytes are enclosed in small cavities within the secreted matrix.

questions.text: Bones have a hard and non-pliable ground substance rich in:
questions.explanation: These give bone its characteristic strength.

questions.text: Bone cells (osteocytes) are present in spaces called:
questions.explanation: Osteocytes are trapped in lacunae within the mineralized matrix.

questions.text: Each muscle is made of many long, cylindrical fibres arranged in parallel arrays. These fibres are composed of numerous fine fibrils called:
questions.explanation: Muscle fibres contract (shorten) in response to stimulation.

questions.text: Skeletal muscle tissue is closely attached to skeletal bones. It is described as:
questions.explanation: Striations are visible and we have conscious control over them.

questions.text: Smooth muscle fibres are fusiform (tapering at both ends) and do not show:
questions.explanation: They are involuntary muscles found in internal organs.

questions.text: Cardiac muscle tissue is a contractile tissue present only in the:
questions.explanation: Intercalated discs allow the cells to contract as a unit.

questions.text: The cells of neural tissue which exert the greatest control over the body's responsiveness to changing conditions are:
questions.explanation: Neurons are the basic excitable units of the neural system.

questions.text: Which cells make up more than one-half the volume of neural tissue in our body and protect neurons?
questions.explanation: Neuroglial cells are non-excitable supporting cells.

questions.text: The body of the cockroach is covered by a hard chitinous:
questions.explanation: Hardened plates called sclerites form the exoskeleton.

questions.text: In cockroach, the head is connected with thorax by a short extension of the prothorax known as the:
questions.explanation: The head is formed by fusion of six segments.

questions.text: The mouth parts of cockroach are of:
questions.explanation: It consists of mandibles, maxillae, labrum and labium.

questions.text: In cockroach, the first pair of wings arises from mesothorax. These forewings are called:
questions.explanation: Tegmina cover the hind wings when at rest.

questions.text: The alimentary canal of cockroach is divided into:
questions.explanation: The foregut includes crop and gizzard.

questions.text: At the junction of midgut and hindgut of cockroach is present a ring of filamentous:
questions.explanation: They remove excretory products from haemolymph.

questions.text: The blood vascular system of cockroach is of:
questions.explanation: Blood vessels open into a space called the haemocoel.

questions.text: Cockroaches are uricotelic, meaning they excrete:
questions.explanation: This is an adaptation for water conservation.

questions.text: The respiratory system of cockroach consists of trachea that open through holes called:
questions.explanation: 10 pairs of spiracles are present laterally.

questions.text: The nervous system of cockroach consists of fused segmentally arranged ________ on the ventral side.
questions.explanation: Three ganglia lie in the thorax, and six in the abdomen.

questions.text: Fertilized eggs of cockroach are encased in capsules called:
questions.explanation: Ootheca is a dark reddish to blackish brown capsule.

questions.text: Development of cockroach is paurometabolous, meaning there is development through:
questions.explanation: Nymphs look like adults and grow by moulting about 13 times.

questions.text: Frog belongs to Phylum Chordata and class:
questions.explanation: Rana tigrina is the most common species of frog found in India.
